"  
Sec. 1  
[The equation involves a fourth-degree polynomial term and an absolute value term. To determine the number of real solutions, we analyze the behavior of the function on both positive and negative values of x. Since the absolute value function is even, we can consider only non-negative values of x and then double the number of solutions found in that domain, unless x equals zero.]

Sec. 2  
[By examining the function for x ≥ 0, we find that it is strictly increasing and crosses the horizontal line y = 7 exactly once. This implies there is one positive solution. Due to the symmetry of the absolute value function, there is also one negative solution. Therefore, the equation has exactly two real number solutions.]
"
